sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of three toe fractures over the past ten years and reports numbness in his right foot, particularly with certain shoes. He states that ibuprofen provides some relief at night but doesn't significantly impact his symptoms during the day. He describes symptoms worsening when performing activities like gardening or moving pots. He reports that his symptoms have remained relatively stable over the past two weeks. The patient rides a bike for exercise and enjoys music-themed rides.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals slight edema and effusion in the right knee. Palpation of the lateral knee reveals no pain. Range of motion is full, with a positive varus test and a negative Lachman's sign. Dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable. X-ray shows no acute fracture or bony abnormality.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient likely has a lateral collateral ligament strain of the right knee. There is no evidence of acute fracture or bony abnormality on the x-ray.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe meloxicam 15 mg once daily. Refer to physical therapy to strengthen the knee and prevent future injuries. Advise the patient to ice the knee as tolerated, 20 minutes on, 20 minutes off, several times daily. Follow up if symptoms worsen or do not improve, or if an MRI is considered.
